codeReview.summary: The implementation fully addresses all expected behaviors: removed the duplicate static checker-briefing.md, updated spec-check-flow.md to reference runtime-generated briefings, and extended all reporting surfaces (emails, channel notifications, CLI) to use canonical proof bundles with explicit work/check/attestation/artifact/doc identifiers. Code is well-structured with proper edge case handling, HTML escaping, and comprehensive test coverage. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: The notificationProofBundle function silently swallows errors when fetching related records (checks, attestations, artifacts, docs). While this is likely intentional for best-effort notification delivery, there's no indication when proof bundles are incomplete, which could make debugging notification issues harder. Consider adding optional debug logging for failed fetches.

summary: PASS - The feature successfully removes duplicate verification docs and aligns committed guidance with runtime behavior. The static checker-briefing.md was deleted, and spec-check-flow.md now explicitly notes that briefings are runtime-generated. All operator-facing reporting surfaces (completion emails, escalation emails, channel notifications, CLI work-show) were extended to include canonical proof-bundle references with explicit IDs for work items, checks, attestations, artifacts, and docs. The ProofBundle type provides a clean shared data structure used consistently across all notification paths. All verification steps passed: baseline tests, focused notify/service/CLI tests, lint, build, and manual inspection confirmed proof bundle references appear in rendered outputs. The implementation fully satisfies VAL-CONTRACT-005, VAL-DOCS-004, and VAL-CROSS-003 requirements.
